_meta.description: Ransomware operator wallets for the risk scorer's ransomware-exposure flag. NOTE: this list is intentionally EMPTY as of v0.34 — see _v034_fabrication_removal. Real ransomware IOCs are collected in BTC/XMR (not Ethereum) and must be sourced from a verified feed (CISA advisories, OFAC SDN), never hand-typed.
_meta.source_caveat: Public attribution of ransomware addresses is messy and infrastructure rotates constantly. Sanctioned ransomware addresses (e.g. DPRK-attributed) now load from the authoritative OFAC SDN feed (ofac_crypto_live.csv). For active investigations, request the latest IOC list from your CISA / FBI liaison.
_meta._v034_fabrication_removal: All 6 prior EVM entries were removed (verify-don't-fabricate sweep): FIVE — LockBit, BlackCat/ALPHV, Royal, REvil, DPRK Maui — were FABRICATED placeholders (hand-typed patterns such as a trailing '...b3e3b3e3b3e3' and descending hex sequences; on-chain eth_getTransactionCount=0 and balance=0, i.e. never transacted). ONE — Conti 0xa910f92acdaf488fa6ef02174fb86208ad7722ba — was a REAL but MIS-ATTRIBUTED high-traffic contract (985,000+ outgoing txs; a ransomware payment EOA never sends ~1M txs), so flagging it severity-4 ransomware would false-positive on legitimate traffic. Ransomware ransoms are paid in BTC/XMR, so EVM 'ransomware payment addresses' were inherently wrong. Repopulate ONLY from a verified source with on-chain-confirmed, correctly-attributed addresses.
